header.headline: Iteration Philosophy
header.subtitle: Use experiments to expose the bottleneck, then promote the lesson into the contract instead of endlessly patching symptoms.

left.title: Diagnosis phase
left.body: Narrow experiments are valuable when they teach you what the representation or curriculum is missing.

right.title: Scaling phase
right.body: Once the lesson is stable, fold it into the broader contract and train on the larger coherent surface.

panels.title: Experiment as sensor
panels.summary: Small runs are most useful when they classify the bottleneck instead of pretending to be the final recipe.
panels.note: A rung is a sensor, not a home.

panels.title: Promote the lesson
panels.summary: The winning move is not endless correction but a better contract, better labels, and broader clean data.

panels.title: Measure again
panels.summary: Harder evaluation keeps the program honest once the surface is easy to memorize.
panels.note: A saturated easy probe tells you to upgrade the question.

callout.title: Key takeaway
points: Iteration only matters if the contract gets stronger.
